text: Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you.

have three clients so far.

Like, how does that typically go, you know?
How do you perform working on your clients?

I founded Litebox about 12 years ago, so we've been in business for a while.
We're a team of 70 people, and although we're 70, like a mid-sized agency, we define ourselves as a boutique agency.
And for us, boutique means that instead of working with 1,000 projects, we work with very few projects to which we dedicate more time.
So we look forward to designing and developing the award-winning kind of website, award-winning kind of digital product, and really high-end stuff.
Something pretty unique about us is that almost every client we have is a startup, so we're very into startups.
But not only that, I would say that probably 90% of our customers are actually either dev tools or high-tech companies.
So I'm going to share with you a couple of examples of websites that we did in the past, or actually recently, from startups.

I really like, Mother Duck, they raised like 200 million, sold, they raised like a seed of 36 million, I think, Code Rabbit, Paid is when we launched, it's just a landing, but we launched it like last week, so it's a new one.
Yeah, Mother Duck is great, I love what you guys did.

Yeah, yeah, that's a fun one, and since Mother Duck, like a lot of companies, came to us, like looking for that experience, but something about Mother Duck is that we did the branding on their website, and we launched their website like two and a half years ago, and since then, we never stopped working, we're in a retainer.
So, what you see is an iteration of, actually, like a lot of iterations of the homepage, and not only that, we released, of course, a whole.
And on the blog, I think it was the first release.
And you can see how many content we have right now.
They have like a learn section, a video section, like a lot of different things.
And we keep adding and adding new stuff.
And not only that, we developed the community that I shared the other day, Octavio Snippets.
So the short story is I was the SEO specialist at OutZero.
Do you know OutZero?
Yeah.
Okay.
So, okay.
They were sold for $6.5 billion, a huge success case.
And I was like the SEO specialist.
There was no other person.
It was a short team.
And something I learned there is that they had an engineering team for their product, but they also had an engineering team for marketing purposes.
So, whatever, like SEO initiative or growth hack, I could.
There was a team ready to tackle it.
They didn't need to reprioritize their backlog or their client feedback.
It was a very separate team.
And I know that a lot of companies can afford to have that in-house.
So we are kind of becoming like they're engineering growth partners.
So we have some clients that, I don't know, they have a VP of marketing or a VP of growth, and they can actually suggest like different growth hacks, but they can't execute them.
But for most of the cases, there's no SEMO.
There's not a head of go-to-market.
They don't have their role.
So we are kind of like taking that protagonism and just like suggest and recommend like different growth hacks and things that we can do together.
So right now we're working with other like high-tech companies such as browser-based, co-pilot kit, like a lot of like the...
The cool kids from the blog, so that definitely makes us proud, but I wanted to mention what's our expertise and what we're doing and how we're helping these type of customers.
Regarding your other question, how does it work, how does us involved in a project might look like?
What we usually do is we assign a team, which usually consists of one UX UI designer, one or two software engineers, and someone from QA.
And we do very high-end stuff, like custom design, custom illustrations, everything custom coded in the next UDS.
But it usually takes much longer than just launching a new website from one week to another.
It usually takes us like about three or four weeks to actually launch a new page.
Okay?
So we can definitely help getting something done quicker.
It will not be as polished as these references that I'm sharing you.
These ones, all of these, they took a lot of work.
So you need to take that into consideration.
And, of course, if you want something less polished and quicker, it will probably not be, like, custom-coded.

I see.
